title: Poinsettia havanensis
taxonomy: Poinsettia havanensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is native to the mountains of eastern Cuba and is closely related to the more well-known Poinsettia plant. The plant is also known as the Cuban Poinsettia and is often used as an ornamental. Poinsettia havanensis is a rare species that is not often cultivated commercially. It is typically found growing at high elevations in the wild.

intro:
Unfortunately, I must inform you that there is no information on the plant Poinsettia havanensis as it is not a recognized scientific name. However, I can provide information on Poinsettia, which is a plant with the scientific name Euphorbia pulcherrima.

Poinsettia (Euphorbia pulcherrima) is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, commonly known as the Christmas star or Christmas flower. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues.

The bioactive constituents of Poinsettia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Poinsettia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.

However, it's essential to note that the effectiveness and safety of Poinsettia as a medicinal plant have not been extensively scientifically studied, and more research is needed to confirm its health benefits and potential interactions with other medications.
